figures.caption: Fig. 1. Robot behavior modeled as a stable flow matching dynamical system (SFMDS) on a Lie group. Velocities ẋt = h1 learned via flow matching. Asymptotic stability is enforced by constraining the solution space of flow matching to a set of admissible velocities ẊA derived from a latent Lyapunov function. SFMDS generates multimodal behaviors, represented by blue and red arrows.

Fig. 5. Vector fields of learned soft (left) and hard (right) SFMDS on LASA. White trajectories denote demonstrations, red trajectories show rollouts from the same initial conditions, and background color indicates normalized speed (lighter colors indicate higher values).
Fig. 10. Ground-truth (GT) and generated trajectories via Soft and Hard SFMDS associated with two different demonstrations ((a) and (b), respectively).
TABLE I ACCURACY AND STABILITY ON THE LASA AND LASA T2 DATASETS.

Fig. 8. Learned SFMDS on the water-pouring task in SE(3). (a)-(b) Flow lines computed at constant orientation and height (white) and demonstrations (cyan) projected onto the xy-plane. Flow lines are initially multimodal (a) and converge at the end (b). Background color indicates the desired cup rotation (low/dark initially, higher/brighter near pouring). (c) Example demonstration (light bottles) and reproduced SFMDS trajectory (dark bottles).

Fig. 7. Vector fields of learned soft (top) and hard (bottom) SFMDS in the Multimodal R2 dataset. White trajectories denote demonstrations, red trajectories show rollouts from the same initial conditions, and background color indicates normalized speed.
Fig. 14. Examples of trajectories generated by Soft and Hard SFMDS when starting from OOD initial states. Out-of-bound pixels are white if below the lower bound, and black if above the upper bound.
Fig. 13. Diagram of the UNet-like architecture used to parameterize uθ , for both soft and hard SFMDS.

Fig. 6. Vector fields of learned soft (left) and hard (right) SFMDS on LASA T2. White trajectories are demonstrations, red trajectories show rollouts from the same initial conditions, and background color indicates normalized speed.
TABLE II ACCURACY AND STABILITY ON MULTIMODAL DATASETS IN R2 AND SE(3)
